title: 23:24 â October 5, 2023
body_text:
23:24 â October 5, 2023
Currency Ordering Updates
The Royal Bank of Canada has updated their submission process for discrepancy cash claims. They have also adjusted their policies regarding contaminated, mutilated, and/or unfit Canadian and foreign currency bank notes.
We updated the information on the submission of discrepancy cash claims and policies on contaminated, mutilated and/or unfit Canadian and foreign currency.
â¢
All discrepancy claims must only be sent via armoured car. Sending discrepancy claims through registered mail is no longer accepted by any of the Royal Bank cash centres.
â¢
Unfit US bank notes can be accepted as regular, fit US bank notes as long as the full bill is present and its value is easily discernable.
â¢
The minimum value a shipment of contaminated and/or mutilated US bank notes must add up to is $100 USD.
â¢
The minimum fee per shipment of contaminated and/or mutilated US bank notes is $50 USD. This fee will vary depending on the total amount of US submitted.
â¢
All non-US foreign currency that is contaminated, mutilated, unfit and/or out of circulation must not be accepted.
â¢
Foreign currency bank notes valued at $0.50 CAD or less and foreign currency coins must also not be accepted.
â¢
The $10 polymer bank note from the Frontier Series (issued in 2013) is now unfit.
We also replaced all âsecure siteâ references with âClient Centreâ and updated all instructions on how to access applications via the Client Centre.
Currency Volume
Currency Ordering Part
Section 10.5, Handling Discrepancies in Incoming Cash Parcels
We added a note that reflects the change in submitting discrepancy cash parcels.
Section 10.6, Handling Counterfeit Bank Notes in Incoming Cash Parcels
We added a note that reflects the change in submitting discrepancy cash parcels
.
Section 11.3, Returning US Bank Notes
We added a note regarding how to determine fit and unfit US bank notes.
Section 11.7, Returning Unfit Canadian Bank Notes
We added a note to reflect that the $10 Frontier Series polymer bank note (issued in 2013) is now unfit.
Section 11.9, Returning Contaminated and/or Mutilated US Bank Notes
We added a note indicating the minimum dollar amount of bank notes that can be returned and the fee calculation for returning these bank notes.
Section 12.1, Overview of Placing Foreign Currency Order â FX Notes Plus
We added a note to provide updated criteria for accepting foreign bank notes and coins (not including US currency).
